acceptance_targets: [CRAFT_UNEARNED_UPTAKE_KAI_KNOWS_MOTIVE] Before snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:183, a cold reader can name which Lena-details Kai has been told and which ones he only suspects. The revised scene fails if Kai still presents Margot's pleasure motive as established fact before the text has shown who gave him that information.

[CRAFT_SANITIZED_ADDICTION_EXPLAINED_NOT_FELT] A reader can point to at least one specific present-tense bodily craving beat in the revised opening or dock scene, not just explanatory sentences about why the feed mattered. The revision fails if Margot still sounds chiefly analytical about the addiction and the page never renders what her body wants right now.
[PROSE_FLAT_DEFAULT_chapter_15] Line 3263 does not use 'flat.' Kai's vocal quality is described in his own register. Margot's 'flat' at 3225 remains the chapter's only facial/vocal 'flat.'
[R005] Ch 15 does not mention grey streaks or grey-streaked hair in reference to Petra.
[R015] Ch 15 contains no more than one instance of 'fluorescent.'
[R033] Ch 15 contains no more than 10 prose lines with em-dashes (≤~8% of 125 prose lines).
[R041] Ch 15 has no more than 26 sentences (≤7% of ~375) beginning with 'She.'

findings.acceptance_test: Before snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:183, a cold reader can name which Lena-details Kai has been told and which ones he only suspects. The revised scene fails if Kai still presents Margot's pleasure motive as established fact before the text has shown who gave him that information.
findings.problem: The Kai-section close third states Margot's pleasure motive and the full Lena-pool betrayal as settled fact before Margot has confessed it on-page. Lena's off-page briefing in line 153 gives logs and suspension, but line 155 jumps to Kai 'knowing' that the dissolution was a rush for Margot; then line 181 has him confronting her with the same conclusion as if the inference chain were already earned. The scene arrives pre-solved, which weakens the confrontation's reveal and breaks local POV-legibility.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:153 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:155 so the text distinguishes what Lena actually told Kai from what he is inferring. Then adjust snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:181 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:183 so Margot's admission that the desync improved the feed is either the first confirmed statement of motive or is explicitly grounded in an earlier quoted disclosure.

findings.acceptance_test: A reader can point to at least one specific present-tense bodily craving beat in the revised opening or dock scene, not just explanatory sentences about why the feed mattered. The revision fails if Margot still sounds chiefly analytical about the addiction and the page never renders what her body wants right now.
findings.problem: The chapter is supposed to expose Margot's feed addiction as ugly, bodily compulsion, but most of the rendering is explanatory or retrospective. The opening gives ritual and numbness, then line 19 says the thing between her and the device is 'already over,' which defuses present addictive pressure. The dock confession explains thresholds, intensity, and lost competition, yet it still reads more like a lucid diagnosis than the immediate misery of wanting the thing that wrecked other people. That is sanitization by summary: the addiction becomes legible without staying physically unbearable on the page.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:15, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:19, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:203, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:207, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:223, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:227 to add a present-time bodily pull toward the interface/feed: compulsive handling, anticipatory body-memory, disgust at the missing response, or another concrete craving beat. If line 19 remains, the surrounding text needs to make clear that the dead pulse is shock or trauma numbness, not the extinction of desire.

findings.acceptance_test: Line 3263 does not use 'flat.' Kai's vocal quality is described in his own register. Margot's 'flat' at 3225 remains the chapter's only facial/vocal 'flat.'

evidence.excerpt: 3225: She looked up when she heard him. Her face was flat. Not composed. Flat. The humor that she usually carried like a blade — the sharp, self-aware darkness that made people uncomfortable when they realized she wasn't entirely joking — was gone. What was left was the face of someone who had stopped constructing anything. She didn't look guilty. She didn't look sorry. She looked like what she was.

3263: "Tell me what it is," Kai said. Not a question. Not exactly a demand. The tone was flat in the way his voice went flat when the humor was completely gone, when there was nothing between the words and the feeling underneath them. "The feed. What it is for you. I want to hear you say it."

rewrite_direction.excerpt: 3263: "Tell me what it is," Kai said. Not a question. Not exactly a demand. The tone was flat in the way his voice went flat when the humor was completely gone, when there was nothing between the words and the feeling underneath them. "The feed. What it is for you. I want to hear you say it."

findings.problem: 'Her face was flat. Not composed. Flat.' (Margot, 3225) and 'The tone was flat in the way his voice went flat' (Kai, 3263). The repetition of 'flat' across two characters in the same chapter dilutes its force. The Ch 15 deliberate emphasis ('Not composed. Flat.') is effective; the subsequent 'flat' for Kai's voice reduces the earlier usage by proximity.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3263, replace Kai's 'flat' with a different word for his specific vocal emptiness: 'stripped,' 'level,' 'emptied.' Keep Margot's 'flat' at 3225, which is deliberately deployed for emphasis.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch 15 does not mention grey streaks or grey-streaked hair in reference to Petra.
findings.evidence: Ch 15 line 3185: 'the grey streaks in her hair, the lines around her eyes, the particular fatigue of someone who had been tired for years.'; Ch 4 line 942: 'The grey streaks in her hair seemed more pronounced'; Ch 7 line 1661: 'grey-streaked and usually pinned with architectural precision'; Ch 14 line 2864: 'gray-streaked hair escaping its pin.'
findings.problem: Petra's grey-streaked hair is described for the fourth time. The Ch 15 instance additionally re-describes her chronic exhaustion, established in Ch 4 line 942 and Ch 7 line 1661. By Ch 15 the reader has encountered 'grey streaks' three times already.
findings.rewrite_direction: Cut the hair-color detail in Ch 15 entirely. The scene's power is Petra's institutional authority. If a physical grounding is needed, use a detail not previously established.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch 15 contains no more than one instance of 'fluorescent.'
findings.evidence: Ch 15 lines 3081, 3185, 3324: three 'fluorescent' instances. The chapter also uses fluorescent as a character-judgment device twice (Margot's mirror, Petra's aging). Manuscript-wide total: 40.
findings.problem: Three fluorescent references in Ch 15. The reader is 15 chapters into a facility-set novel and no longer needs fluorescent lighting re-established.
findings.rewrite_direction: Keep at most one; the mirror scene (line 3081) where Margot confronts her reflection is the strongest. Cut the other two.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch 15 contains no more than 10 prose lines with em-dashes (≤~8% of 125 prose lines).
findings.evidence: Ch 15: 35/125 prose lines contain em-dashes = 28.0%. Manuscript-wide: 654/1876 = 34.9% (threshold 10%, target <8%).
findings.problem: Ch 15 has em-dash density of 28.0%, far exceeding the 10% threshold. The em-dash is used for parenthetical asides, appositives, and mid-sentence pivots at a rate that flattens the prose rhythm. Manuscript target: reduce from 654 to <150 em-dash lines.
findings.rewrite_direction: Reduce em-dash-containing lines in Ch 15 from 35 to ~10 (25 cuts). Convert parenthetical em-dash asides to commas, semicolons, colons, periods, or restructured sentences. Reserve em-dashes for their highest-impact uses: dramatic interruptions, voice-specific rhythms, and moments where the break creates meaning.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch 15 has no more than 26 sentences (≤7% of ~375) beginning with 'She.'
findings.problem: Ch 15 has 'She'-opener density of 12.0%, well above the 7% threshold. This creates a rhythmic monotony where consecutive sentences beginning with 'She' flatten the prose into a subject-verb march.
findings.rewrite_direction: Reduce 'She'-opener sentences in Ch 15 from 45 to ~26 (19 rewrites). Vary sentence openings: lead with setting, sensory detail, dependent clauses, object-first constructions, or dialogue attributions.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel asks what it means to own a body that has been partially replaced, optimized, or networked. The answer cannot be abstract. The physical horror of enhancement failure — sensory blackouts, autonomous muscle firing, personality thinning — must be rendered with visceral specificity. The intimacy between enhanced people must confront the fact that their bodies are partly engineered, and the strangeness of that must be present on the page, not elided. Margot's addiction to the sensory feed must be shown as a genuine addiction with genuine physical craving, not as an interesting character trait. Tomas's paralysis must be ugly, specific, and permanent — not a tidy plot device. The corporate betrayal must feel institutional, not melodramatic. When the novel asks its readers to sit with discomfort — a body that moves without consent, a mind that thins to nothing, an addiction that destroys someone else — the prose must stay present and not retreat to safety. The specific is always braver than the general.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism at the point of consequence is a craft failure. When a body crashes into a wall at sprint speed, the reader should feel the impact — bone, skin, blood, the specific geometry of injury. When Margot feels Tomas's spinal impact through the feed, the sensation must be rendered in full: the compression, the silence of nerves going dead, the phantom that stays after she disconnects. When Kai's body moves without his consent during sex, the reader must experience the uncanny specificity of reflexes that are not his. When Lena loses time, the gap must feel real — not a graceful ellipsis but a genuine absence. Every scene where the novel touches difficult material — bodily horror, addiction, institutional betrayal, the vulnerability of intimacy — must be rendered with the same specificity given to any other moment. Retreat into summary, metaphor, or tasteful vagueness at these moments destroys the novel's central claim that bodies matter.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters in spoken English. Uncontracted forms are reserved for emphasis, deliberate formality, or character-specific moments (Lena's slightly formal second-language register, Petra's clinical precision). Any uncontracted line in casual dialogue should be checked: does this character have a reason to be formal here?

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate high-intensity chapters with reflective or quiet ones. Vary scene count, dialogue density, and narrative distance across consecutive chapters. A chapter driven by confrontation should not be followed by another confrontation. A chapter heavy in dialogue should be followed by one that relies more on interiority and physical description. The quiet chapters (7, 16, parts of 18) should not feel like connective tissue between the 'real' scenes — they carry the human baseline that makes crisis meaningful. Some chapters should be physically compressed (a single long scene) and others structurally looser (multiple shorter scenes with white space between them).
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary literary — technically precise when inhabiting engineering or medical perspectives, grounded in body experience when inhabiting athletic or intimate perspectives. The enhancement technology should be described through felt sensation, not through exposition. How a neural link feels, not how it works. The novel's primary sensory vocabulary is the body: muscle, bone, nerve, heat, pressure, the experience of a body doing things its owner did not command.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Purple proprioception — the sensory descriptions of enhancement should remain specific and physical, not drift into poetic abstraction about the nature of consciousness
Exposition dumps about how the technology works — establish through character experience, not through narrator explanation
Uniform crisis register — not every chapter should feel urgent; some should feel ordinary, empty, or quiet, and the prose should match
Philosophical narrator — the narration should not editorialize about the body-autonomy theme; the events carry the argument
Symmetrical emotional beats — not every character should have matching epiphanies or matching moments of clarity; some characters end muddled
Mechanical metaphors for human experience — the point is that these people are not machines, even when their bodies partly are; do not let the prose treat them as systems
Identical blackout descriptions — each sensory disruption scene must find different language for the experience rather than reusing void/static/white metaphors
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, rotating POV by chapter. The narration inhabits each POV character's sensory and cognitive world — what they notice, how they process sensation, what language they would use internally. The narration should not be smarter, more articulate, or more philosophically aware than the character whose head we are in. Ensemble chapters may shift between characters within scenes but must establish clear transitions.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Variable across chapters and within chapters. Technical/calibration scenes: precise, measured, slightly clinical. Sensory/feed scenes: fractured, overlapping, synaesthetic. Interpersonal scenes: looser, more conversational, allowing silence and dead space. Action/blackout scenes: compressed, fragmented, physical. Quiet scenes: unhurried, sensory, present tense of ordinary experience. No single rhythm should dominate the novel.
